specification.topic-statement: The message kind classifier determines whether a message kind string represents a conversation turn that should be included in requests to a language model.

scope.in-scope: Classification of a kind string as a conversation kind or a non-conversation kind.

behavior.classifying-a-kind:
A kind string is provided. If the string is absent, the result is false. If the string exactly matches one of the six recognized conversation kinds, the result is true. Any other string produces false.
The six recognized conversation kinds are: - "system" - "user" - "assistant" - "tool_call" - "tool_result" - "tool"
The kind "tool" is treated as a conversation kind. It is annotated in the implementation as a legacy or wire-format message type, distinct from the current "tool_call" and "tool_result" kinds.
All other kind strings — representing metadata events, agent lifecycle events, UI events, and any unrecognized values — produce false.
